seo.title: E-Commerce Code Generation AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for code generation AI agents in e-commerce. Covers FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS for payments.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents that generate and execute code in e-commerce environments introduces compounded risk: a single hallucinated pricing function or misconfigured checkout snippet can trigger FTC enforcement, PCI DSS violations, and mass customer refund disputes simultaneously. This checklist addresses the intersection of code generation safety and e-commerce regulatory compliance, ensuring every generated artifact is safe to execute, legally sound, and commercially accurate. Teams that skip these controls risk not just technical failures but brand-destroying incidents that erode customer trust and invite regulatory scrutiny.

sections.heading: Code Safety & Sandbox Execution Controls
sections.description: Ensure all LLM-generated code is validated, sandboxed, and tested before touching production e-commerce systems, preventing malicious or erroneous code from affecting live transactions.

items.label: Enforce Isolated Sandbox Execution
items.description: Run all LLM-generated code in a network-isolated, resource-limited sandbox (e.g., gVisor, Firecracker) with no access to production databases, payment processors, or customer PII before human or automated approval.

items.label: Static Analysis Before Execution
items.description: Run generated code through a static analysis tool (e.g., Bandit for Python, ESLint security plugins for JS) to detect injection vulnerabilities, hardcoded secrets, and unsafe eval() or exec() patterns before sandbox execution.

items.label: Dependency Vulnerability Scanning
items.description: Scan all third-party packages referenced in generated code against CVE databases (e.g., Snyk, OWASP Dependency-Check) and block installation of packages with known critical vulnerabilities or typosquatting patterns.

items.label: Automated Test Suite Validation
items.description: Require generated code to pass a predefined test suite covering edge cases for pricing calculations, discount logic, and cart operations before promotion to staging; fail the pipeline if test coverage drops below 80%.

items.label: Iterative Refinement Loop Limits
items.description: Cap the number of autonomous refinement iterations at a defined threshold (e.g., 5 cycles) to prevent runaway agent loops; escalate to human review if the agent cannot produce passing code within the limit.

items.label: Output Diff Review for Production Deployments
items.description: Generate a human-readable diff of all code changes proposed by the agent and require explicit engineer sign-off before any deployment to staging or production environments.

items.label: Execution Resource Quotas
items.description: Enforce CPU, memory, and execution time limits on sandbox runs to prevent denial-of-service conditions and detect infinite loops or resource exhaustion bugs in generated code.

sections.heading: Credential & Data Leakage Prevention
sections.description: Prevent the AI agent from embedding, logging, or transmitting sensitive credentials, customer PII, or payment data within generated code artifacts or execution logs.

items.label: Secret Detection Pre-Commit Scan
items.description: Integrate a secret scanning tool (e.g., TruffleHog, GitGuardian) into the code generation pipeline to automatically reject any output containing API keys, database connection strings, OAuth tokens, or PCI-scoped card data patterns.

items.label: Prohibit PII in Code Generation Prompts
items.description: Sanitize all inputs to the LLM agent to strip real customer names, email addresses, order IDs, and payment card numbers before submission; use synthetic or tokenized data for all code generation and testing contexts.

items.label: Log Redaction for Sensitive Fields
items.description: Configure sandbox execution logs to automatically redact patterns matching credit card numbers (PAN), CVV, SSN, and email addresses before logs are written to any storage system, satisfying PCI DSS Requirement 3.4.

items.label: Environment Variable Injection Policy
items.description: Mandate that all credentials referenced in generated code use environment variable injection patterns (e.g., os.environ.get()) and block any code that hardcodes credentials, even in test files.

items.label: Agent Memory Isolation Between Sessions
items.description: Ensure the LLM agent's context window and memory store are cleared between unrelated code generation sessions to prevent cross-contamination of customer data or business logic from prior tasks.

items.label: Outbound Network Restriction in Sandbox
items.description: Block all outbound HTTP/S requests from the sandbox execution environment except to an approved allowlist of internal test endpoints, preventing exfiltration of injected test data.

items.label: Audit Trail for All Generated Artifacts
items.description: Maintain an immutable audit log of every code artifact generated, including the prompt hash, model version, timestamp, and approving engineer, to support incident investigation and regulatory audit requests.

sections.heading: E-Commerce Regulatory Compliance in Generated Code
sections.description: Ensure that code generated by the AI agent faithfully implements pricing, product claims, return policies, and accessibility requirements as mandated by FTC guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, and ADA standards.

items.label: Pricing Logic Accuracy Validation
items.description: After generating any pricing, discount, or promotional code, run automated tests with known inputs and expected outputs to verify calculations match the approved pricing rules; flag any discrepancy greater than $0.01 for human review before deployment.

items.label: Return & Refund Policy Representation Check
items.description: Require a compliance review step for any generated code that renders or processes return/refund policy text, ensuring the displayed policy matches the legally approved version stored in the policy repository, not a hallucinated summary.

items.label: ADA Accessibility Compliance for UI Code
items.description: Run all generated front-end code through an automated accessibility checker (e.g., axe-core, Lighthouse) and enforce WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, including proper ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, and color contrast ratios, before merging.

items.label: FTC Advertising Claim Substantiation Gate
items.description: Block deployment of any generated code that dynamically renders product claims, testimonials, or promotional badges (e.g., '#1 Rated', 'Clinically Proven') without a linked substantiation reference validated against the marketing compliance database.

items.label: CCPA/GDPR Consent Flow Integrity
items.description: Validate that any generated code modifying cookie consent banners, data collection forms, or opt-out flows preserves all required consent signals and does not introduce dark patterns that obscure user rights under CCPA or GDPR.

items.label: PCI DSS Scope Boundary Enforcement
items.description: Automatically reject generated code that introduces direct handling of raw card data (PAN, CVV, expiry) outside of the approved tokenization or iframe-based payment component, maintaining PCI DSS scope boundaries.

items.label: Tax Calculation Code Review
items.description: Flag any generated code that implements tax rate logic for manual review by a tax compliance specialist before deployment, as incorrect tax calculations create legal liability across jurisdictions.

items.label: Promotional Terms & Conditions Linkage
items.description: Verify that generated code rendering promotional offers (flash sales, BOGO, coupons) includes a visible, clickable link to the full terms and conditions, as required by FTC promotional advertising guidelines.

sections.heading: Monitoring, Incident Response & Governance
sections.description: Establish ongoing monitoring, rollback capabilities, and governance processes to detect and remediate failures in AI-generated code before they cause customer harm or regulatory violations.

items.label: Real-Time Pricing Anomaly Detection
items.description: Deploy a monitoring rule that alerts within 60 seconds if any product price deviates more than a defined threshold (e.g., 20%) from the baseline catalog price after a code deployment, enabling rapid rollback before widespread customer exposure.

items.label: Automated Rollback on Test Failure in Production
items.description: Configure the deployment pipeline to automatically revert to the last known-good version if synthetic transaction tests (e.g., add-to-cart, checkout, refund) fail within 5 minutes of a production deployment of AI-generated code.

items.label: Human-in-the-Loop Approval for Payment Code
items.description: Require mandatory human engineer review and approval for any AI-generated code that touches checkout flows, payment processing integrations, or order management systems, regardless of automated test pass rates.

items.label: Model Version & Prompt Logging for Reproducibility
items.description: Log the exact LLM model version, temperature settings, and full prompt used for every code generation task so that any deployed artifact can be reproduced, audited, or challenged during an FTC investigation or customer dispute.

items.label: Quarterly Red Team Exercises for Agent Jailbreaking
items.description: Conduct quarterly adversarial testing where a security team attempts to manipulate the code generation agent into producing code that bypasses pricing rules, leaks credentials, or disables consent mechanisms.

items.label: Customer-Facing Error Rate SLA Monitoring
items.description: Track error rates on pages and APIs modified by AI-generated code against a defined SLA (e.g., <0.5% 5xx errors); trigger an incident response workflow automatically if the threshold is breached post-deployment.

items.label: Refund Dispute Correlation Dashboard
items.description: Build a dashboard correlating spikes in refund disputes or chargeback rates with recent AI-generated code deployments to quickly identify causation and support rollback decisions.

items.label: Annual Compliance Review of Agent Guardrails
items.description: Schedule an annual review of all code generation guardrails, blocklists, and compliance rules with legal, security, and engineering stakeholders to incorporate regulatory updates and lessons from incidents.

notes.note: All bespoke and custom software, including AI-generated code, must follow a secure software development lifecycle. Requirement 6.3.2 mandates maintaining an inventory of all bespoke software; ensure AI-generated code artifacts are catalogued with version history and are subject to the same vulnerability review process as human-authored code.

Any AI-generated code that dynamically renders product claims, star ratings, or promotional language must ensure those claims are substantiated and not misleading. The FTC has signaled increased scrutiny of AI-generated marketing content; generated code that surfaces unsubstantiated superlatives (e.g., 'best price guaranteed') without verification logic creates direct enforcement exposure.

notes.regulation: CCPA (Cal. Civ. Code §1798) & GDPR Article 25 — Privacy by Design
notes.note: Code generated for data collection forms, analytics integrations, or personalization features must implement privacy by design principles. Any AI-generated code that introduces new data collection points must be reviewed to ensure opt-in/opt-out mechanisms are preserved, data minimization is respected, and no new cross-context behavioral advertising signals are created without proper disclosure.

E-commerce platforms are subject to ADA Title III obligations for digital accessibility. AI-generated front-end code must not degrade existing accessibility compliance; courts have consistently held that inaccessible checkout flows constitute discrimination. Automated accessibility testing must be a blocking gate in the CI/CD pipeline for all AI-generated UI components.

cta.heading: Automate Your E-Commerce Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously enforce these controls across every AI-generated code deployment, turning this checklist into automated guardrails that protect your revenue, your customers, and your regulatory standing.
